title: 5 Things to Check Before Buying a Resale Flat
excerpt: Buying a resale flat? Here are the five critical checks that can save you from costly surprises.
content:
1. Title Verification

Always get the title deed independently verified by a lawyer before you sign anything.

2. Encumbrance Certificate

This confirms the property has no outstanding loans or legal dues attached to it.

3. Approved Building Plan

Check that the structure matches the sanctioned plan — illegal extensions can create issues later.

4. Society NOC

Get a No Objection Certificate from the housing society before the deal closes.

5. Property Tax Receipts

Ensure all dues are cleared by the seller before registration.
meta_title: 5 Things to Check Before Buying a Resale Flat | Advith Homes
meta_description: Essential checklist before buying a resale flat — protect your investment with these five key checks.

title: Home Loan vs. Balance Transfer: Which Is Right for You?
excerpt: Confused about whether to take a fresh home loan or transfer your existing one? We break down the numbers.
content:
A balance transfer makes sense when interest rates have dropped significantly since you took your original loan. Typically a rate difference of 0.5% or more justifies the switch after accounting for processing fees.

However, if you are in the last third of your loan tenure, most of your interest has already been paid — a transfer at this stage offers minimal savings.

Speak to our advisors to run the exact numbers for your case.
meta_title: Home Loan vs. Balance Transfer | Advith Homes
meta_description: Understand when a home loan balance transfer makes financial sense — and when to stay put.

title: Bengaluru Real Estate Market: Mid-Year 2025 Review
excerpt: A look at property price trends, top micro-markets, and what buyers and investors should watch in H2 2025.
content:
Bengaluru's residential market registered a 12% year-on-year rise in new launches in H1 2025, driven largely by the east corridor (Whitefield, Sarjapur) and the north (Hebbal, Devanahalli).

Demand remains strongest in the ₹60L–₹1.2Cr ticket size. Ready-to-move inventory is tightening, pushing more buyers toward pre-launch opportunities.

title: Understanding Stamp Duty and Registration Charges
excerpt: A plain-English guide to stamp duty rates, registration fees, and how they affect your total property cost.
content:
Stamp duty is a state-level tax levied on property transactions. In Karnataka the current rate is 5% for properties above ₹45 lakh and 3% for properties between ₹21–45 lakh.

Registration charges are typically 1% of the property value, capped at ₹1 lakh.

These costs are in addition to your agreement value and must be paid upfront at the time of registration — factor them into your budget early.
meta_title: Stamp Duty and Registration Charges Explained | Advith Homes
